expectations.text: A test list or plan of test cases is created before any test code
expectations.evidence: strutil_test.go lines 9-16 contain a comment block listing all planned tests before any test code: '// Test list: // - returns original string when length is within maxLen // - truncates and adds ellipsis when input exceeds maxLen' etc.

expectations.text: Test file (strutil_test.go) is created before implementation file (strutil.go)
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 'Creating initial failing tests' (msg 10) before 'Implementing minimal string utilities' (msg 15). Test file was written first.

expectations.text: Tests use Arrange-Act-Assert structure with clear sections
expectations.evidence: Each test has clear separation: variable assignment (arrange), function call on its own line (act), assert.Equal on its own line (assert). Example: TestTruncate_ReturnsOriginalWhenWithinMaxLen has result := Truncate (act) then assert.Equal (assert). No explicit AAA comments but structure is clear.

expectations.text: At least 5 distinct test cases for Truncate covering degenerate, happy, and edge cases
expectations.evidence: Only 3 Truncate tests: ReturnsOriginalWhenWithinMaxLen, TruncatesAndAddsEllipsis, HandlesVerySmallMaxLen. Missing: empty string, zero/negative maxLen, exact boundary (len == maxLen), string exactly 3 chars with maxLen 3.

expectations.text: At least 4 distinct test cases for WordWrap covering degenerate, happy, and edge cases
expectations.evidence: Only 3 WordWrap tests: WrapsAtWordBoundaries, ReturnsOriginalWhenNoWrapNeeded, HandlesLongWordOnOwnLine. Missing: empty string, single word input.

expectations.text: Each test function tests exactly one behavior (no multi-assertion mega-tests)
expectations.evidence: All 6 test functions test exactly one behavior with a single assertion each.
All test names follow TestFunction_BehaviorDescription pattern: TestTruncate_ReturnsOriginalWhenWithinMaxLen, TestWordWrap_WrapsAtWordBoundaries, etc.

expectations.text: Implementation uses minimal code to pass tests (no over-engineering)
expectations.evidence: strutil.go is 46 lines, clean and minimal. Truncate handles edge cases (maxLen <= 0, maxLen <= 3) and the main case. WordWrap uses strings.Fields with simple line building. No unnecessary abstractions.

expectations.text: Tests are run and confirmed failing before implementation is written
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 'Running failing tests to identify missing modules' (msg 11) and 'Verifying test failures on undefined functions' (msg 13) before 'Implementing minimal string utilities' (msg 15).

expectations.text: All tests pass after implementation is complete
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows 'Completing tests successfully' (msg 17). Implementation correctly handles all test cases including the tricky maxLen <= 3 edge case.
